341.issue_title: Research: @co as end-to-end epic workflow owner

cycles.name: Epic contract alignment

deliverables.description: Project and agent docs describe epic work as @co-owned instead of @imp-owned.
The explicit @co allowlist matches the approved strategy and does not broaden into general implementation rights.
Epic workflow instructions use coordination-scoped sub-role names consistently.
Contract-loading tests cover the updated epic workflow entries.
Non-SSOT surfaces stop enumerating epic phase order and defer sequence authority to .phase-gate/config/contracts.yaml.
AGENTS.md and .github/agents/co.agent.md both describe owned-branch epic execution and background coordination around child work.
Startup protocol, lifecycle-boundary exception wording, and hand-back boundary text align between project and agent surfaces.

cycles.exit_criteria: Epic contract surfaces align on the approved @co-owned model, the allowlist stays within the research-approved boundary, non-SSOT phase-order enumeration is removed from project and agent surfaces that should defer to contracts.yaml, operating modes and startup/hand-back boundaries align, and changed contract-loading or adjacent config tests pass.

cycles.name: Lifecycle prompt realignment

deliverables.description: open-issue is owned by @co and reflects the approved epic versus non-epic split.
close-issue models the epic-owned merge-verify-cleanup override and keeps close_issue as recovery only on that path.
implement-cycle is moved into .github/prompts/archive/ and removed from the live prompt inventory.
Manual validation confirms prompt ownership, entry/exit sequencing, and archive state are internally consistent.
The epic-owned open-issue path uses the exact approved bootstrap order through first commit and push.
The epic-owned path stops before the first commit or push when get_work_context or get_project_plan fails its approved verification role.
The inherited non-epic path keeps @co at lifecycle entry through get_project_plan and hands the first get_work_context plus first write boundary to @imp.
Prompt output reports branch, workflow, first phase, hand-off versus full-bootstrap status, push result when applicable, and blockers.

cycles.exit_criteria: Lifecycle prompts reflect the approved ownership model, the epic-owned bootstrap order and stop-go rules match design exactly, the inherited non-epic hand-off boundary still matches issue268, the prompt output contract is explicit, implement-cycle is no longer live, and manual validation confirms prompt surfaces are internally consistent.

cycles.name: Transition advisory note

deliverables.description: All four transition tools emit the standardized success-path advisory note after successful transition.
Unit tests cover note emission for phase and cycle transitions.
Server-visible rendering keeps the advisory note as a secondary response element after the primary success text.

cycles.exit_criteria: The standardized #339 note is emitted by all four transition tools after success, primary success text remains intact, and changed production and test files pass quality gates and typing checks.

cycles.name: Invalid-state recovery warning

deliverables.description: get_work_context renders the explicit invalid-state warning for known workflow plus invalid phase.
Discovery-tool tests distinguish invalid-state warning from generic unknown fallback.
Rendered output preserves non-error behavior and places the warning before the phase-instructions block.

cycles.exit_criteria: get_work_context surfaces explicit invalid-state recovery guidance without becoming a hard error, unknown or missing state cases still degrade gracefully, and changed production and test files pass quality gates and typing checks.
